• About
  • Blog
  • Brock

I’ve been looking for an easy way to get a blog up and running to keep track of some of the projects I’ve been working on. I stumbled onto Jekyll while reading the Learn Enough CSS & Layout Tutorial. After reading a bit on Jekyll there I decided to give it a try. I had to pick my way through the tutorail to find the pieces I needed to get a simple blog up and running.

I decided to build my Jekyll project in a Cloud 9 environment. I’ve used Cloud 9 a lot recently for learning Ruby on Rails and for working through some problem sets for Harvard’s CS50 course, and I really like it. The Jekyll project is running in a blank workspace, which comes with Ruby 2.4.0 by default. All I had to do was install Jekyll, and I was ready to get started with a new project.

Built using Jekyll
Hosted on GitHub Pages
